items.title: India orders temporary ban on Telegram over exam fraud concerns
items.summary: The restrictions include a nationwide ban on Telegram until June 22 and a requirement to disable the app's message editing feature.

items.title: This startup’s super metals could soon be in military drones, luxury watches, and chef’s knives
items.summary: Instead of heating metals, Foundation Alloy beats them into submission. The startup has raised $22 million to scale up production of its alloys.

items.title: SpaceX to acquire Cursor for $60B in stock, days after blockbuster IPO
items.summary: The deal is supposed to help SpaceX's struggling AI division. The company told IPO investors it sees a $26 trillion addressable market in AI.

items.title: Threads adds new personalization and community features as it reaches 500M monthly users
items.summary: The Meta-owned social platform announced a series of new features launching today, including a "Your Algo" tool that lets users control what they see in their feeds

items.title: ChatGPT’s market share slips below 50% for first time
items.summary: The chatbot still remains the most popular AI assistant worldwide with over 1.1 billion monthly users, followed by Gemini with 662 million and Claude with 245 million.

items.title: Malaysia’s AI agent-powered messaging app Respond.io raises $62.5M, eyes acquisitions
items.summary: Respond.io, one of Malaysia startups to watch, uses AI agents to handle high volumes of customer inquiries and charges per convo, not per seat.

items.title: Sundar Pichai faces boos, walkout at Stanford graduation ceremony over Google’s Israel, ICE ties
items.summary: AI is once again at the heart of a college graduation protest — this time for the technology's use in Google's defense contracts.

items.title: The US government’s Anthropic models ban was never about an AI jailbreak
items.summary: The Trump administration's decision that forced Anthropic to pull its latest cybersecurity models could be reactionary, retaliatory, or both, but the message is clear: The AI industry isn't immune from U.S. government interference.

items.title: Meta’s new ‘AI Mode’ on Facebook pulls from public info across its platforms
items.summary: Meta announced Monday that it's rolling out a wave of new AI features on Facebook, the latest sign of the company's effort to catch up in the AI race and keep users more engaged on the platform.

items.title: SpaceX is public: Everything you need to know post-IPO
items.summary: TechCrunch has followed SpaceX's start, struggles, and successes from the early days. And we're here for what happens next too. This package of SpaceX IPO coverage includes who stands to win (and maybe some who won't), pre-IPO deals, and what's tucked inside its S-1 registration document.

items.title: These are the countries moving to ban social media for children
items.summary: Australia was the first country to issue a ban in late 2025, aiming to reduce the pressures and risks that young users may face on social media, including cyberbullying, social media addiction, and exposure to predators.

items.title: Cybersecurity vets protest ‘dangerous’ US government ban on Anthropic’s most powerful models
items.summary: A group made up of dozens of cybersecurity experts urged the White House to remove export-control restrictions on Anthropic’s Fable and Mythos models, arguing that the order is going to limit the ability of cybersecurity defenders to secure their software and products.

items.title: SpaceX’s biggest-ever IPO just grew to $85.7 billion raised
items.summary: SpaceX's IPO underwriters maxed out their share purchases, adding to an already historic amount of money raised.

items.title: UK unveils sweeping social media ban for users under 16
items.summary: The ban would apply to a range of social media platforms, including Snapchat, TikTok, YouTube, Instagram, Facebook, and X.

items.title: Salesforce acquires AI customer service platform Fin for $3.6B
items.summary: Salesforce says it wants to use Fin's team and technology to improve Agentforce, its existing enterprise platform that businesses can use to build custom AI agents that automate tasks.

items.title: Sarvam becomes India’s newest AI unicorn with $234 million funding round led by HCLTech
items.summary: Indian IT services company HCLTech is investing $150 million in the Bengaluru startup.

items.title: Fox to acquire Roku in $22B deal
items.summary: Fox says the deal will create the third-largest television company in the United States.

items.title: As AI agents become employees, NewCore emerges with $66M to give them identities
items.summary: NewCore argues the next challenge in enterprise security will be managing AI agents, not people.

items.title: A satellite just learned to find things on its own — here’s what that means
items.summary: In April, for the first time ever, an Earth observation satellite found what it was looking for, all on its own.

items.title: The AI layoff wave is becoming a powder keg
items.summary: At the very moment that tens of thousands of workers are being shown the door, a small cohort of AI insiders is becoming wealthy on a scale that's hard to comprehend.
